attack_chain.description: Threat actors used an old, unrecycled AWS access key that had not been rotated/invalidated to gain unauthorized access to a Juspay server.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Threat actors used an old, unrecycled Amazon Web Services (AWS) access key to gain unauthorized access to the server

evidence_chunks.quote: Threat actors used an old, unrecycled Amazon Web Services (AWS) access key to gain unauthorized access to the server
Threat actors used an old, unrecycled Amazon Web Services (AWS) access key to gain unauthorized access to the server

attack_chain.description: Attacker accessed one of Juspay's data stores, performing discovery/activity that consumed unusual system resources.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: alerted by unauthorized activity in one of the data stores

evidence_chunks.quote: alerted by unauthorized activity in one of the data stores
alerted by unauthorized activity in one of the data stores

attack_chain.description: Threat actors accessed and collected approximately 35 million records of masked card data, card fingerprints, email IDs and phone numbers from the data store.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: threat actors breached about 35 million records with 'masked card data and card fingerprint'

attack_chain.description: The stolen data was exfiltrated and ultimately dumped online approximately five months after the breach.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Data from a breach that occurred five months ago involving Juspay ... has been dumped online

initial_access_vector: Compromised old, unrecycled AWS access key used to access a Juspay data store server
persistence_mechanism: None identified; intrusion was stopped and server terminated upon detection
privilege_escalation: None identified; existing AWS access key already had sufficient privileges to access the data store
